response.justification: Candidate has strong general software engineering experience and notable geospatial domain background through GISCollective and Green Map, which aligns with the routing/navigation context. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ (or Rust) development, and the resume shows only a brief 6-month C/C++ stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, with no Rust experience. Primary expertise is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. Worth a screening call to probe systems-programming depth and willingness to ramp on C++, but technical fit is weak as-is.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map)
Distributed/low-latency systems exposure at Dunnhumby (DLang, async I/O, high-throughput services)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tooling
concerns: No modern C++ experience; only brief, dated C/C++ work from 2014-2015
No demonstrated navigation, routing, or map-tiling pipeline work
